model_menu_line_1: Choose an existing profile or configure the shared Telegram profile.
model_menu_line_2: Existing profiles are preferred when you already have working profiles configured locally.
no_existing_profiles_line_1: No configured profiles are currently available.
no_existing_profiles_line_2: Use the shared-profile flow to create one from Telegram, or add profiles locally first.
model_setup_cancelled_line_1: Cancelled the Telegram model configuration flow.
model_setup_cancelled_line_2: Send /model to open the model menu again.
send_text_for_current_step: Send text for the current step or use the buttons below.
custom_base_url_invalid: Base URL must be a valid http(s) URL.
api_key_received: API key received. Review before saving.
no_flow_active_line_1: No Telegram model configuration flow is active.
expired_session_line_1: This Telegram model configuration session expired.
expired_session_line_2: Send /model to open the model menu again.
send_custom_base_url: Send the custom base URL as a plain text message.
send_custom_model: Send the model id as a plain text message.
send_api_key_next: Send the API key as the next message.
existing_profile_unavailable_line_1: The selected profile is no longer available.
no_remaining_default_profile: No remaining default profile was available.
api_key_saved: API key saved into mono local secrets.
choose_existing_profiles: Choose one of the existing configured profiles.
waiting_existing_selection: Waiting for existing Telegram profile selection
review_existing_line: Choose whether to enable this profile or remove it.
waiting_existing_review: Waiting for Telegram existing profile confirmation
choose_api_type: Choose the API type to configure.
third_party_note: All three support third-party compatible URLs. The examples below use official endpoints.
waiting_api_type: Waiting for Telegram API type selection
official_or_custom_url: You can use the official URL or send any third-party compatible URL.
waiting_base_url_selection: Waiting for Telegram base URL selection
send_custom_base_url_now: Send the custom base URL now.
waiting_custom_base_url_input: Waiting for custom Telegram base URL input
use_suggested_or_custom_model: Use the suggested model or send a custom model id.
send_custom_model_now: Send the custom model id now.
waiting_custom_model_input: Waiting for custom Telegram model id input
warning_not_secure: Warning: Telegram is not a high-security secret channel.
key_stored_locally: The key will be stored in mono local secrets on this machine.
delete_message_after_save: After you save, mono will try to delete your Telegram API key message.
waiting_api_key_confirmation: Waiting for Telegram API key confirmation
send_api_key_now: Send the API key as the next message.
delete_message_after_save_short: mono will store it locally and try to delete the Telegram message after you save.
waiting_api_key_input: Waiting for Telegram API key input
save_shared_profile_line: Save to update the shared Telegram model profile and set it as the default profile.
waiting_shared_save: Waiting for Telegram shared profile save confirmation
pair_help_step_1: 1. Configure the bot token: mono telegram token <BOT_TOKEN>
pair_help_step_2: 2. Start mono TUI so the Telegram control runtime is polling.
pair_help_step_3: 3. A new user DMs the bot and receives a pairing code.
pair_help_step_4: 4. Approve from the platform with /pair telegram code <CODE>.
pair_help_step_5: 5. The user is added to the Telegram DM allowlist store.
pair_help_footer: Use /telegram status to inspect the active Telegram configuration.
dm_pairing_default: DM pairing is the default policy. Unknown Telegram DM senders receive a short pairing code.
group_allowlist_config: Group allowlists are configured under mono.channels.telegram.groups in ~/.mono/config.json.
use_model_help: Use /model in an approved DM to choose an existing profile or configure the shared Telegram profile.
use_help_line: Use /help to list the available commands.
if_tui_running: If mono TUI is running, you can message the bot now.
approved_help_footer: Use /help to see the available Telegram control commands.
pairing_not_configured: mono Telegram access is not configured.
ask_owner_to_approve: Ask the owner to approve with:
group_allowed: This group is already allowed by configuration.
group_not_allowed: This group is not allowlisted yet. Add it under mono.channels.telegram.groups in ~/.mono/config.json.
profile_list_changed: The profile list changed. Please choose again.
